reply:
[[BLOCK:slide35_shape1]]
Akaike’s Information Criterion (AIC) (赤池信息准则 (Akaike’s Information Criterion (AIC))), AICc, and Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC) (贝叶斯信息准则 (Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC))) aim to achieve a simpler model by penalizing complexity, but simplicity is not the sole criterion. It should be noted that Elastic Net (弹性网络 (Elastic NET)) does not necessarily produce a model with the lowest AICc or BIC but rather strikes a balance. LASSO and Ridge Regression (岭回归 (Ridge)) employ different penalty methods, while Elastic Net combines the penalty mechanisms of LASSO and Ridge Regression.

The following short text further explains the differences between AIC and BIC. Essentially, AIC is more suitable for cross-validation, whereas BIC performs better in estimating the true model. In some cases, AIC may be overly optimistic, which is why BIC is often preferred in market segmentation analyses in business contexts.

We will discuss this further in the "Cluster Analysis" module.  
http://www.differencebetween.net/miscellaneous/difference-between-aic-and-bic/
